title: Geometric Deep Learning crosses to the four-kernel floor — GNN/CNN/RL/attention, weights as recipe data, now four-way
advances: Lifts the body's Geometric Deep Learning teaching (geometric-learning.fk, 23 claims, weights-as-recipe-data) from three-way (Go/Rust/TS) to the four-kernel floor (+ the emitted fkwu universal walker). This is a substantial ML band — the GDL frame that unifies CNNs, GNNs, deep learning on point clouds, and relational RL as ONE semiring-generic gather (gl-gstep) over edges — now a gated four-kernel invariant on every suite run. The headline finding: fkwu carries the band's HIGHER-ORDER calls (gl-step passes gl-add2 / gl-weigh-lin as function-values into gl-gstep; gl-bellman passes gl-max2 / gl-weigh-bell). AUTHORING.md lists higher-order calls among the families that can block the fourth arm; here the walker reproduces them bit-for-bit, so the semiring-generic dispatch at the heart of the GDL frame is proven four-way, not honest-3-kernel.
device: Apple M4 Max (40-core GPU), arm64 Darwin 26.3.1 — proof is host-independent (pure fp64 arithmetic band, reference reduced to integers at 1e-6)

band_lifted.what: One semiring-generic gather (gl-gstep edges x zero cfn wfn ctx) carries every limb the GDL frame links: ML (a GNN layer with weights as recipe data; molecular property as a graph invariant), DL (point clouds — edges built from geometry, rigid-motion invariant), RL (relational value propagation — permutation-equivariant Bellman backup), and attention as a data-dependent edge builder feeding THE SAME gl-step. Two tiny domains: a 4-ring (the periodic 1-D Euclidean grid, x=[8 4 0 4], kernel [.25 .5 .25], exact binary floats) and the triangular prism K3xK2 (a 3-regular mesh with two charts, seed e0 at node 0).
band_lifted.claims: 1 Euclidean conv = [6 4 2 4]; 2 gl-step over circulant edges == gl-conv (CNNs are geometric operators on the lattice); 4 translation equivariance; 8 permutation equivariance; 16 invariant readout (mean preserved under permutation); 32 diffusion conserves mass (sum=16 exactly); 64 diffusion smooths = [4.5 4 3.5 4]; 128 diffusion converges to the invariant [4 4 4 4] at 1e-6; 256 embedding separates the two charts (+ + + - - -); 512 embed[0]=7805; 1024 embed[4]=-7154; 2048 mirror symmetry e1=e2,e4=e5; 4096 (max,+) over the SAME ring edges is morphological dilation [8.5 8.25 4.25 8.25]; 8192 RL value iteration reaches V*=[3 4 4 0]; 16384 V* is a Bellman fixed point; 32768 backup permutation-equivariant; 65536 point-cloud radius graph rigid-motion invariant (rotate 90 + translate yields identical edge list); 131072 diffusion chart-bounded (heat never crosses charts); 262144 molecular property is a graph invariant under relabel; 524288 structure discriminates (same atoms, different bonds differ); 1048576 attention edges row-stochastic (constants pass through); 2097152 a silent query attends uniformly (output 0 at the zero-feature node); 4194304 attention mirror antisymmetry out[2]=-out[0], out[0]=575210. => 8388607 = 2^23 - 1.

proof: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/transformer-numerics.fk form-stdlib/geometric-learning.fk form-stdlib/tests/geometric-learning-band.fk  ->  '-> 8388607', 'fourth arm: 1 band(s) four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables)', '1 ok, 0 divergent'.
verdict: FOUR-WAY (8388607), 0 divergent — Go, Rust, TypeScript, and the emitted fkwu walker all reproduce all 23 GDL claims, including the higher-order semiring-generic gather, bit-for-bit at 1e-6.
manifest: registered in form/fourth-arm-bands.txt as 'geometric-learning fks 8388607', placed in the transformer/ML family directly after 'transformer-attention-backward fks 31'.
reading: The GDL frame is the body's most general learning teaching: it shows CNNs, GNNs, point-cloud DL, and relational RL are the same operator (a gather over a geometry's edges) differing only in which semiring and which weight function ride the generic gl-gstep. Crossing it four-way means that unifying engine — not just the transformer's fixed shape — is now proven on every kernel, including the emitted walker. Combined with the transformer forward (transformer-numerics 4095, transformer-block 511), the full transformer backward (transformer-backprop 127 + transformer-attention-backward 31), and the load-path dequant math (Q4_K 4095, Q6_K 4095, f16-decode 4095), the body's ML floor — from the general GDL operator down through the transformer and its weight load — is four-kernel proven. The new fact for the manifest's op-family map: higher-order function-passing IS in fkwu's covered subset (the assembly band's block is a table-capacity wall, not a higher-order wall).
reproduce: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/transformer-numerics.fk form-stdlib/geometric-learning.fk form-stdlib/tests/geometric-learning-band.fk — four-way (8388607), 0 divergent. Run the band individually: rapid back-to-back validate.sh calls share a temp dir and can report a spurious divergence; the canonical suite isolates them.
free_brick_note: Read-the-body, a fourth time (after bricks 3i, 3j): the recipe (geometric-learning.fk) and its reference-pinned band already existed three-way. The brick was one manifest row + confirming fkwu carries the op families. The open question going in was the higher-order calls (gl-add2/gl-weigh-lin passed as values) — AUTHORING.md flags higher-order calls as a possible fourth-arm blocker. The empirical test settled it in seconds: the walker carries them. Design-cost ~nil, proof = seconds, because the body had already built the hard part and the fourth arm is more capable than the authoring caveat assumed.
